page: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.8xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8488C processor operating at 3.2 GHz. The configuration provides 32 virtual CPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. System memory consists of 128.0 GB of DDR5 RAM running at 5 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

The underlying Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and Advanced Vector Extensions. In performance testing, the instance demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, scoring in the top 25% for Geekbench single-core, image processing, and text processing benchmarks. Multi-core performance, measured via stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests, falls within the middle 50% average. Cryptographic operations and database transaction simulations also align with average tier expectations.

The 5 GHz DDR5 memory subsystem delivers strong large-block write bandwidth, placing in the top 25% of tested configurations, while read bandwidth remains in the average tier. This memory profile supports efficient data compression, with single-threaded gzip compression performing in the top 25%. For machine learning workloads, the hardware-assisted matrix instructions enable top-tier text generation speeds and strong prompt processing for small 135M parameter language models. However, performance scales down on larger models, with text generation for a 70B parameter model falling into the bottom 25% due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration.

The m7i-flex.8xlarge offers a balanced resource profile suitable for general-purpose workloads, web serving, and moderate-scale data processing. Its static web serving throughput is highly competitive, ranking in the top 25%. The flex instance family provides a cost-efficient alternative to standard instances by leveraging variable compute performance for workloads that do not require constant maximum CPU utilization. It is suited for application servers, containerized microservices, software compilation, and small-scale machine learning inference.
description: The Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, but lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates strong performance in single-core tasks, image processing, and small-scale LLM text generation, where it reaches top-tier speeds. However, multi-core processing and large-block memory reads remain in the average tier, and large-scale LLM generation is weak due to the absence of GPU hardware. This instance is designed for cost-efficient general-purpose workloads, including web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 32 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering balanced performance for web serving and moderate database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.8xlarge features 32 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual server featuring DDR5 memory and hardware-accelerated execution for web serving and moderate compute workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 32-vCPU Intel Xeon 8488C processor at 3.2 GHz
Equipped with 128.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Features Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated vector math
Delivers strong performance in static web serving and small-model LLM inference
Lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware
Best suited for web servers, application hosting, and software compilation
